links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is one of the only two trogon species found in the Caribbean. It is the national bird of Haiti

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The Hispaniolan trogon is a forest-dependent species and is vulnerable to habitat loss or degradation. The IUCN Red List conservation status of the Hispaniolan trogon is assessed as Least Concern; however, its population is continuing to decline throughout its small range, owing to forest degradation and fragmentation. Forest loss and fragmentation owing to shifting agriculture are causing a decline, particularly in moist forest areas
